title: Sphaeropteris fugax
taxonomy: Sphaeropteris fugax is classified within the division Pteridophyta, which comprises of ferns and their allies. It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida, a group of ferns characterized by having true roots and vascular tissue. The order is Polypodiales, which includes a wide range of ferns with various growth habits. Sphaeropteris fugax is further classified within the family Cyathea, a group of tree ferns native to the tropical and subtropical regions. At the species level, Sphaeropteris fugax is a unique and relatively rare species within its genus.

intro: Sphaeropteris fugax, also known as the Fugax Tree Fern, is a species of tree fern native to tropical regions. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Sphaeropteris fugax include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, from digestive issues to skin problems.
